what: Use tl.assume(M>0) and tl.assume(N>0) and tl.assume(K>0) in custom_kernel to help Triton compiler optimize; change M=64 waves_per_eu from 1 to 2 (v80 tested with cache_modifier=None which confounded the result); keep .cg cache modifier
why: v80 tested waves_per_eu=2 + cache_modifier=None together and got neutral result, but cache_modifier=None independently hurts (v193). waves_per_eu=2 alone with .cg preserved has never been tested for M=64. The library tuned config for AFP4WFP4 uses waves_per_eu=1 for M<=64 N=2112 K=7168 but the preshuffle kernel has different memory access patterns.

what: .wt cache modifier on M=256 quant scale stores (from .cg) — write-through bypasses L2 write allocation for scale data consumed by ASM GEMM
why: 24+ BM tests confirm -2-3% on M=256. Previous LB attempts always showed M=256 improvement but LB noise on M<=64 shapes masked it at geomean level. Now with v201 baseline (waves_per_eu=2 for M=64), M=64 may be more stable, reducing noise floor.
override: New baseline v201; previous attempts were all on v188 baseline; M=64 may be more stable with waves_per_eu=2

what: M=32 K<=1024: change waves_per_eu from 2 to 0 (let compiler decide) — compiler may find better occupancy balance for BSM=8 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2 .cg
why: v201 proved waves_per_eu change alone can give improvement (M=64 from 1 to 2 kept). For M=32 K<=1024, waves_per_eu=2 was set in v099 as part of a larger change. waves_per_eu=0 for M=32 K<=1024 with BSM=8 BSN=128 was never tested alone.

what: Split-K path: change waves_per_eu from 2 to 1 for the 16x2112x7168 shape — reduce occupancy hint to allow larger register file per wavefront
why: Current split-K uses waves_per_eu=2, matching the M=32 K>1024 config that was set in v096. The library tuned A16WFP4_PRESHUFFLED config for N=2112 K=7168 uses waves_per_eu=1 for all M ranges. v201 showed waves_per_eu changes can improve scores. Testing waves_per_eu=1 for split-K path.
